name: Oyster Global Employment API
what_it_does: Oyster HR API for global employment and employer-of-record (EOR) services. Enables programmatic access to employee data, payroll records, benefits information, and hiring workflows across 180+ countries. Helps companies hire internationally without setting up local entities.
use_cases: Querying global employee records and employment status from HR automation agents
Retrieving payroll processing data and timelines from payroll reporting agents
Monitoring onboarding status across countries from HR workflow agents
Accessing benefits enrollment data from employee experience agents
not_for: Domestic-only HR (Oyster is optimized for international employment)
High-volume manufacturing or gig worker payroll at scale
best_when: A company using Oyster to hire internationally wants AI agents to query employee data, payroll status, and onboarding workflows across multiple countries.
avoid_when: You need autonomous employment or payroll changes. PRIVACY RISK: Global employee data includes highly sensitive PII across international jurisdictions with varying legal requirements.

auth.notes: Oyster API uses API keys and OAuth. Contact Oyster for developer access. Partner API available for HRIS integrations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Oyster EOR pricing per employee per month
pricing.paid_tiers: Employee pricing varies by country and employment type
pricing.notes: Oyster charges per international employee. API access included with service contract.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not publicly documented

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY RISK: International employee PII across 180+ countries with varying legal requirements
No public MCP server — requires REST API integration
Employment and payroll changes require human legal and HR oversight
GDPR and local labor law compliance required for data access
API access requires Oyster service contract and approval
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY RISK: Global employee PII, payroll data, benefits information. SOC2 and GDPR. Cross-border data handling requires legal compliance per jurisdiction.

security_score_components.security_notes: Highly sensitive international employee data. SOC2 and GDPR. Cross-jurisdictional data handling. Employment changes require human oversight.
